title: Functions & 'Jeet Kune Do' Logic
philosophy: Bruce Lee's Jeet Kune Do emphasizes 'maximum efficiency with minimum effort.' Functions embody this: write once, strike (execute) infinitely.
economics: A function is a social contract or a specialized factory. It takes raw materials (inputs/arguments), processes them through labor (logic), and outputs a finished good.
techconcept: Functions are defined using the 'def' keyword. They bundle reusable logic under a single name and can take parameters and return values.
lesson: Instead of repeating the same code many times, you define a function once and call it whenever you need that behavior. Clear function names and small focused bodies make your programs easier to test, debug, and extend.
challenge: What keyword is used to define a function in Python?
hint: Think of the first word you type before the function name.
